diagrams.title: Node Structure in Singly Linked List
diagrams.description: Shows how each node contains data and pointer to next node
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the basic building block of singly linked lists - each node stores data and a reference to the next node in sequence

diagrams.title: Insertion at Beginning
diagrams.description: Step-by-step visualization of adding node at start of list
elements: Arrow from new node to old head
Head pointer update to new node
After: HEAD -> NEW -> A -> B -> NULL
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how insertion at beginning works - creates O(1) operation by just redirecting head pointer

diagrams.title: Traversal Flow
diagrams.description: Shows sequential access from head through all nodes to NULL
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how we access elements - must follow links sequentially from head to desired position

diagrams.title: Node Deletion Process
diagrams.description: Visualizes removing a node from middle of list
diagrams.purpose: Understanding deletion: bypass the node by updating previous node's next pointer to skip the deleted node
